item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In like it, but a truck handing out free guns in the American southwest and no florida man is taking one, that ain't makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is basically a boogaloo boi who hates that ocean corpse collecting thingy

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Late to the party, but if you didn't read the entirety of the brochure in the voice of the Soldier from Team Fortress 2, you're doing yourself a disservice.

Convenient link to a video with example of said voice, for reference.

It's exactly the sort of manic psuedo-military devotee of hyper-violence the scip is presenting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I completely agree. Is there some sort of memetic or otherwise anomalous effect in play here? Because even if it was a creepy dead guy, I'd still be stocking the bed of my truck with as much as it could carry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept of an undead gun nut that either doesn't understand his powers, doesn't care about how they work, or both, manically unloading weapons on the general public to fight in some unseen war just makes me feel all warm and fuzzy inside. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the best self-insert I've ever seen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who wouldn't take all of this guys weapons?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, y'know what? This is my favorite article ever. I literally don't have a single thing to change here except that I want to see SO MUCH MORE of this guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I relate to this crazy, gun distributing, car dealership commercial-evoking, zombie on a personal level. Well, I at least like him a lot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually don't get it, mind explaining?

What has come? To die? Why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's my guess on the overall theme:

+ Show
- Hide

This guy is, in a sense, the Horseman of War. But no one wants to approach him because, well, he's a profanity screaming zombie handing out illegal guns.

Also, I'm super surprised but quite delighted that the Foundation hasn't attempted to actively test the rewards thing. I don't know if it was simple common sense or the Ethics Committee, but I think it's a better overall story in the end because of it. I know some writers might go into a detailed experiment log on the subject, but that just begs the question of "why would they even test that?"

+1. Fun read, short and sweet, amusing document included.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MUTHER MULITIA BABY!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
This made me smile hard, however, I call bullshit on the section below. Free automatic weapons AND flame throwers. Wouldn't pass that up in a second. <3

As SCP-3884's activities invariably attract local law enforcement attention, its attempts to supply weaponry to the populace are brief and swiftly interrupted. To date, civilians have reacted negatively to all SCP-3884 appearances and none have willingly accepted any items given by SCP-3884-01. As a result, none have participated in its rewards program, thus information regarding the aforementioned “prizes” is limited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has an unbalanced*, manic over the topness that appeals to me. Gunmetal Gary - his prices are crazy and so is he! Maximum Ragnarok 'n' roll with the finest of anomalous weaponry!

*The character and situation, not the writing. The writing is fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: at the very least, gary appears to know something about it. but chances are he's not talking

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait wait, this is connected to all that underwater stuff and the big end-of-the-world thingy? o.o (Mother)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering that this is physically uncontainable at the moment and capable of causing mass casualties, I think this should probably be classified as Keter over Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: all things eventually, friend. give me time to build

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I got that (and the Joey Fucknuts tale), but I haven't been to able see any ANSWERS to the Boss' identity in them (unless I'm blind and there's something I'm missing).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it probably has something to do with this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
KEEP YOUR GUTS WHERE THE BIG BOSS PUT EM!

So, who is this "Boss"? God? The Sorrow? Someone new?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
100,000 points – You are beyond war. Your time has come.

Upvoted.

item.title: lock and load, citizens
item.content:encoded:
my twelfth item, a scared and confused dead man that knows far more than he is capable of comprehending.

thanks to everyone in chat that helped me go over this.

and thank you, reader, for playing along. ♥

image credit

second image from the US air force, in the public domain.
